Name: Suzanne Elizabeth Petersen
Relevant information: On 5 May 2026, a banning order was made under subparagraphs 73ZN(2)(a)(i) and 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013, prohibiting Suzanne Elizabeth Petersen from providing supports and services to persons with disability in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS). Specifically, the 5-year order prohibits Suzanne Elizabeth Petersen from being engaged in the following NDIS activities: • Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any support or supports under a Participant’s Plan; • Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any service or services under a Participant’s Plan; This order takes effect from 5.00pm AEST on 26 May 2026 and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Rim Tlaiss
Relevant information: On 5 May 2026, a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(b) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Rim Tlaiss from: • providing or personally being involved in the provision, to any NDIS participant, of any supports or services (whether paid or unpaid), under a participant’s plan; or• engaging in any work or providing or performing any supports or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider.‘NDIS Participant’ means a participant within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act.‘NDIS Provider’ means an NDIS Provider within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act.‘Participant’s Plan’ means a plan within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act.This banning order takes effect on 26 May 2026 at 5.00pm for a period of two years and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Leanne Lawton
Relevant information: On 6 May 2026, a banning order was made under section 73ZN (2)(a)(i) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013, prohibiting Leanne Lawton from being engaged in the following:1. Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any support or supports under a Participant’s Plan; 2. Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any service or services under a Participant’s Plan;3. Engage in any work or provide or perform any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider, which may occasion any personal contact between you and an NDIS Participant. Note: Personal contact refers to direct interaction between persons, which includes (but is not limited to) bodily contact with another person, or where persons are in the same physical location where bodily contact is possible. Personal contact also includes (but is not limited to) communications, such as physically speaking to another person, speaking over the phone, emailing, writing a letter, or sending text messages. NDIS Participant means a participant within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act; NDIS Provider means an NDIS Provider within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act; Participant’s Plan means a plan within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act This banning order takes effect on 26 May 2026 at 5.00pm for a period of one (1) year and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Mon Cheri Gifts Pty Ltd T/A Diversity Models
Relevant information: On 27 April 2026, a banning order was made under section 73ZN(1)(a) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Mon Cheri Gifts Pty Ltd T/A Diversity Models from engaging in the following NDIS activities: 1. Engaging in activities that a of a National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) provider would undertake, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services in the registered or unregistered market. 2. Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by a consultant for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services in the registered or unregistered market. 3. Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by an auditor for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services in the registered or unregistered market. 4. Engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports of services to people with disability in the NDIS in the registered or unregistered market. 5. Engaging in the provision or management of NDIS supports, services or funding to people with disability in the NDIS. 6. Providing or being involved or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS participant, of any support or supports under an NDIS participant’s plan. 7. Engaging in any work or providing or performing any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS provider, which may occasion any personal contact between Mon Cheri Gifts Pty Ltd and an NDIS participant. Name: Luapule Filipo-Saipani
Relevant information: On 15 March 2026 a banning order was made under sections 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Luapule Filipo-Saipani for a period of five (5) years from: • Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any support or supports under a Participant’s Plan; • Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any service or services under a Participant’s Plan; • Engage in any work or provide or perform any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider, which may occasion any personal contact between you and an NDIS Participant. Relevant information: On 2 March 2026 a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(b) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Sandra Lee Ashton for a period of three (3) years from: 1. In this instance: • 'contact' means face-to face contact, oral communication, written communication or electronic communication, where you are physically touching the participant, speaking with, or building a rapport with the participant • ‘unsupervised’ means not requiring direct continuous supervision by a supervisor or senior colleague • NDIS Participant means a participant within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act • NDIS Provider means an NDIS Provider within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act, and • Participant’s Plan means a plan within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act. The banning order takes effect at 5pm on 12 March 2026 and operates across all states and territories of Australia.

Name: Christine Coop
Relevant information: On 27 August 2025 a permanent banning order was made under section 73ZN(1)(a)(i) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 (Cth), effective on 19 September 2025 at 5.00pm across all States and Territories of Australia. On 20 February 2026, the banning order was varied under section 100 of the NDIS Act, permanently prohibiting Ms Christine Coop from engaging in the provision of NDIS supports and services to people with disability in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), including: a) Providing or being involved or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any support or supports under a Participant’s Plan; b) Providing or being involved or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any service or services under a Participant’s Plan;c) Engaging in any work or providing or performing any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider, which may occasion any personal contact between you and a NDIS Participant. Further information: The banning order was made on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner reasonably believes that Christine Coop contravened section 73V of the NDIS Act by, in connection with providing NDIS supports and services, “failing to provide supports and services in a safe and competent manner with care and skill”; and by “failing to take all reasonable steps to prevent and respond to all forms of violence against, and exploitation, neglect and abuse of people with disability.”, as required under section 6(1)(d) of the NDIS (Code of Conduct) Rules. This variation of the banning order is effective from 5:00pm on 25 February 2026 and remains permanently in effect across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: El Gracious Care and Services Pty Ltd, trading as EL GRACIOUS CARE AND SERVICES PTY LTD
Registration Groups: 107 assistance with daily personal activities; 108 assistance with travel/transport arrangements; 115 assistance with daily life tasks in a group or shared living arrangement; 116 innovative community participation; 117 development of daily living and life skills; 120 household tasks; 125 participation in community, social and civic activities; 136 group and centre-based activities
Relevant information: El Gracious Care and Services Pty Ltd can no longer operate as registered NDIS provider, including providing specialist behaviour support, specialist disability accommodation, plan management services, any class of support or service to a person whose NDIS plan is managed by the NDIA and implementing regulated restrictive practices.Further information:On 19 January 2026 a notice of revocation of registration was issued to El Gracious Care and Services Pty Ltd under section 73P(1)(a) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 (NDIS Act). The revocation takes effect from 18 February 2026.  El Gracious Care and Services Pty Ltd can no longer operate as registered NDIS provider, including providing specialist behaviour support, specialist disability accommodation, plan management services, any class of support or service to a person whose NDIS plan is managed by the NDIA and implementing regulated restrictive practices. The revocation decision was made on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner reasonably believes that El Gracious Care and Services Pty Ltd has contravened, is contravening, or is proposing to contravene the NDIS Act. In forming this reasonable belief, the delegate had regard to outcomes of an audit undertaken of El Gracious Care and Services Pty Ltd and which identified a number of major non-conformities with applicable NDIS Practice Standards and which El Gracious Care and Services Pty Ltd is alleged to have failed to rectify.

Name: Enable Occupational Therapy In Mental Health Pty ltd
Relevant information: On 16 February 2026 a permanent banning order was made under section 73ZN(1)(a) and section 73ZN(1)(b)(i) of the NDIS Act 2013. Specifically, the order prohibits Enable Occupational Therapy in Mental Health Pty Ltd from being engaged in the provision or management of NDIS supports, services or funding to people with a disability in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), including but not limited to: a) Engaging in activities of, and as, a NDIS provider; b) Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by a consultant for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; c) Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by an auditor for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; and/or d) Engaging in activities in a non-service delivery and non-support delivery role for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or services to people with disability in the NDIS. This banning order takes effect at 5:00pm AEST on 17 February 2026 for a permanent period and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Enable Occupational Therapy In Mental Health Pty Ltd
Registration Groups: 106 assistance in coordinating or managing life stages, transitions and supports; 107 assistance with daily personal activities; 110 specialist positive behaviour support; 115 assistance with daily life tasks in a group or shared living arrangement; 117 development of daily living and life skills; 118 early intervention supports for early childhood; 125 participation in community, social and civic activities; 128 therapeutic supports
Relevant information: Enable Occupational Therapy in Mental Health Pty Ltd must cease all activities associated with registration as a registered NDIS provider and must not represent itself as a registered NDIS provider, as at the date the revocation takes effect. Enable Occupational Therapy in Mental Health Pty Ltd will no longer be able to provide supports and services to any NDIS participant whose plan is managed by the National Disability Insurance Agency. It will also no longer be able to provide supports and services that require registration as set out in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018, such as: • the provision of specialist behaviour support services, • the provision of supports and services involve the likely implementation of restrictive practices,• the provision of specialist disability accommodation, and• the provision of supports and services related to NDIS plan funding managementDetails: Revocation of registration made on the ground of contraventions of the NDIS Code of Conduct (Rules) 2018. Date of effect: 16 February 2026Further information: The revocation of registration was made on the grounds of contraventions of the NDIS Code of Conduct (Rules) 2018, specifically:Section 6(1)(c) by “failing to provide supports and services in a safe & competent manner with care and skill”; and Section 6(1)(f) by “failing to take all reasonable steps to prevent and respond to all forms of violence against, and exploitation, neglect and abuse of people with disability.

Name: Mete CUKUR
Relevant information: On 4 February 2026 a banning order was made under sections 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) and 73ZN(2)(b) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Mete Cukur for a period of (4) four years from:• Providing or being involved or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any supports and/or services under a Participant’s Plan and which: a. involves unsupervised contact with NDIS participants, whether in a NDIS participant's home or elsewhere and b. involves you accessing or receiving a NDIS participant's personal or financial information. In this instance: • 'contact' means face-to-face contact, oral communication, written communication or electronic communication, where you are physically touching the participant, speaking with, or building a rapport with the participant • ‘unsupervised’ means NOT requiring direct continuous supervision by a supervisor or senior colleague • NDIS Participant means a participant within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act • NDIS Provider means a NDIS Provider within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act, and • Participant’s Plan means a plan within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act. This banning order is effective from 5:00pm AEDT on 13 February 2026 and operates across all states and territories of Australia.

Name: Jerelyn TEMPLE
Relevant information: On 10 February 2026 a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(i) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Jerelyn Temple from being involved in the provision of supports or services to NDIS participants.Specifically, the order prohibits Jerelyn Temple from being engaged in the following NDIS activities: a) Provision of any support or supports under a Participant’s Plan. b) Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any service or services under a Participant’s Plan. c) Engage in any work or provide or perform any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider, which may occasion any personal contact between you and an NDIS Participant. Personal contact refers to direct interaction between persons, which includes (but is not limited to) bodily contact with another person, or where persons are in the same physical location where bodily contact is possible.Personal contact also includes (but is not limited to) communications, such as physically speaking to another person, speaking over the phone, emailing, writing a letter, or sending text messages.NDIS Participant means a participant within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act;NDIS Provider means an NDIS Provider within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act;Participant’s Plan means a plan within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act This banning order takes effect on 10 February 2026 at 5:00pm for a period of one year and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Holy Trinity Disability Services Pty Ltd, t/a HOLY TRINITY DISABILITY SERVICES
Relevant information: Holy Trinity Disability Services Pty Ltd, t/a HOLY TRINITY DISABILITY SERVICES is required to take action to address alleged non-compliance with section 73J of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013. More specifically, Holy Trinity Disability Services Pty Ltd, t/a HOLY TRINITY DISABILITY SERVICES is required by 20/02/2026 to take action to engage an approved quality audit to complete a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and by 30/04/2026 to provide the NDIS Commission a copy of the completed mid-term audit report. Date and time of effect: 30/01/2026 Further information: The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was satisfied that Holy Trinity Disability Services Pty Ltd, t/a HOLY TRINITY DISABILITY SERVICES, has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, by allegedly failing to commence a mid-term audit within 18 months the registration coming into force and failing to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed under section 73G of the NDIS Act.

More specifically, The Trustee for Peoples Point of Care Trust, t/a The Trustee for PEOPLES POINT OF CARE TRUST is required by 20/02/2026 to take action to engage an approved quality audit to complete a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and by 30/04/2026 to provide the NDIS Commission a copy of the completed mid-term audit report. Date and time of effect: 30/01/2026 Further information: The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was satisfied that The Trustee for Peoples Point of Care Trust, t/a The Trustee for PEOPLES POINT OF CARE TRUST, has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, by allegedly failing to commence a mid-term audit within 18 months the registration coming into force and failing to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed under section 73G of the NDIS Act.

More specifically, Merciful Heart Care Pty Ltd, t/a MERCIFUL HEART CARE PTY LTD is required by 20/02/2026 to take action to engage an approved quality audit to complete a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and by 30/04/2026 to provide the NDIS Commission a copy of the completed mid-term audit report. Date and time of effect: 30/01/2026 Further information: The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was satisfied that Merciful Heart Care Pty Ltd, t/a MERCIFUL HEART CARE PTY LTD, has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, by allegedly failing to commence a mid-term audit within 18 months the registration coming into force and failing to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed under section 73G of the NDIS Act.

More specifically, Loving Gentle Care Pty Ltd, t/a LOVING GENTLE CARE PTY LTD is required by 20/02/2026 to take action to engage an approved quality audit to complete a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and by 30/04/2026 to provide the NDIS Commission a copy of the completed mid-term audit report. Date and time of effect: 30/01/2026 Further information: The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was satisfied that Loving Gentle Care Pty Ltd, t/a LOVING GENTLE CARE PTY LTD, has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, by allegedly failing to commence a mid-term audit within 18 months the registration coming into force and failing to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed under section 73G of the NDIS Act.

More specifically, Onefocus Support Services Pty Ltd, t/a ONEFOCUS SUPPORT SERVICES PTY LTD is required by 20/02/2026 to take action to engage an approved quality audit to complete a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and by 30/04/2026 to provide the NDIS Commission a copy of the completed mid-term audit report. Date and time of effect: 30/01/2026 Further information: The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was satisfied that Onefocus Support Services Pty Ltd, t/a ONEFOCUS SUPPORT SERVICES PTY LTD, has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, by allegedly failing to commence a mid-term audit within 18 months the registration coming into force and failing to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed under section 73G of the NDIS Act.

More specifically, EA PHARMACY CONSULTING PTY LTD, t/a ALL CARE ASSIST is required by 20/02/2026 to take action to engage an approved quality audit to complete a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and by 30/04/2026 to provide the NDIS Commission a copy of the completed mid-term audit report. Date and time of effect: 30/01/2026 Further information: The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was satisfied that EA PHARMACY CONSULTING PTY LTD, t/a ALL CARE ASSIST, has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, by allegedly failing to commence a mid-term audit within 18 months the registration coming into force and failing to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed under section 73G of the NDIS Act.

More specifically, Kora Care Pty Ltd, t/a KORA CARE PTY LTD is required by 20/02/2026 to take action to engage an approved quality audit to complete a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and by 30/04/2026 to provide the NDIS Commission a copy of the completed mid-term audit report. Date and time of effect: 30/01/2026 Further information: The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was satisfied that Kora Care Pty Ltd, t/a KORA CARE PTY LTD, has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, by allegedly failing to commence a mid-term audit within 18 months the registration coming into force and failing to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed under section 73G of the NDIS Act.

More specifically, Ms Aissata Camara, t/a AUSTRALIA HOME CARE PROVIDER is required by 20/02/2026 to take action to engage an approved quality audit to complete a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and by 30/04/2026 to provide the NDIS Commission a copy of the completed mid-term audit report. Date and time of effect: 30/01/2026 Further information: The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was satisfied that Ms Aissata Camara, t/a AUSTRALIA HOME CARE PROVIDER, has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, by allegedly failing to commence a mid-term audit within 18 months the registration coming into force and failing to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed under section 73G of the NDIS Act.

More specifically, ELEVATION DISABILITY SERVICES PTY LTD, t/a ELEVATION DISABILITY SERVICES PTY LTD is required by 20/02/2026 to take action to engage an approved quality audit to complete a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and by 30/04/2026 to provide the NDIS Commission a copy of the completed mid-term audit report. Date and time of effect: 30/01/2026 Further information: The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was satisfied that ELEVATION DISABILITY SERVICES PTY LTD, t/a ELEVATION DISABILITY SERVICES PTY LTD, has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, by allegedly failing to commence a mid-term audit within 18 months the registration coming into force and failing to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed under section 73G of the NDIS Act.

More specifically, Marney Leeson Pty Ltd, t/a Kids Physio Newcastle is required by 20/02/2026 to take action to engage an approved quality audit to complete a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and by 30/04/2026 to provide the NDIS Commission a copy of the completed mid-term audit report. Date and time of effect: 30/01/2026 Further information: The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was satisfied that Marney Leeson Pty Ltd, t/a Kids Physio Newcastle, has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, by allegedly failing to commence a mid-term audit within 18 months the registration coming into force and failing to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed under section 73G of the NDIS Act.

Name: Arlie Gibson
Relevant information: On 14 January 2026 a permanent banning order was made under section 73ZN(1)(b)(i) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Arlie Gibson from engaging in the provision or management of NDIS supports, services or funding to people with disability in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) for a period of permanently, including but not limited to: : a. Delivering supports or services to NDIS funded participants; b. Engaging in activities that a member of the key personnel of a NDIS provider would undertake, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or service; c. Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by a consultant for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or service; d. Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by an auditor for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; and e. Engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or services to people with disability in the NDIS. This banning order takes effect at 5.00pm AEST on 29 January 2026 for a permanent period and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Felicity BRAZHER
Relevant information: On 19 January 2026 a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Felicity Brazher from providing, being involved in or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any supports and/or services under a Participant’s Plan and which: a. Involves contact with NDIS participants, whether in a NDIS participant's home or elsewhere; or b. Involves accessing or receiving a NDIS participant's personal or financial information. In this instance: • 'Contact' means face-to face contact, oral communication, written communication or electronic communication, where you are physically touching the participant, speaking with, or building a rapport with the participant and • NDIS Participant means a participant within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act and • NDIS Provider means a NDIS Provider within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act and • Participant’s Plan means a plan within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act The banning order is for a period of five (5) years effective from 5:00pm 28 January 2026. The banning order operates across all states and territories of Australia. The decision to make a banning order was made having regard to Felicity BRAZHER’s relevant criminal history.

The order is effective from 5:00pm AEDT on 28 January 2026 and operates across all states and territories of Australia.

Name: Auspicare Pty Ltd
Registration Groups: 107 assistance with daily personal activities; 108 assistance with travel/transport arrangements; 114 community nursing care; 115 assistance with daily life tasks in a group or shared living arrangement; 117 development of daily living and life skills; 120 household tasks; 125 participation in community, social and civic activities; 136 group and centre-based activities
Relevant information: On 22 December 2025 a notice of revocation of registration was issued to Auspicare Pty Ltd under section 73P(1)(a) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 (NDIS Act). The revocation takes effect from 19 January 2026.  Auspicare Pty Ltd can no longer operate as registered NDIS provider, including providing specialist behaviour support, specialist disability accommodation, plan management services, any class of support or service to a person whose NDIS plan is managed by the NDIA and implementing regulated restrictive practices. The revocation decision was made on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner reasonably believes that Auspicare Pty Ltd has contravened, is contravening, or is proposing to contravene the NDIS Act. In forming this reasonable belief, the delegate had regard to outcomes of an audit undertaken of Auspicare Pty Ltd and which identified a number of major non-conformities with applicable NDIS Practice Standards and which Auspicare Pty Ltd is alleged to have failed to rectify.

Relevant information: On 6 January 2026, a banning order was made under subparagraph 73ZN(2)(a)(i) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Vasiti Natutuvuli a.k.a. Korocawiri from providing supports and services to persons with disability in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS). Specifically, the order temporarily prohibits Vasiti Natutuvuli a.k.a. Korocawiri from being engaged in the following NDIS activities: a) engaging in activities that a member of the key personnel of an NDIS provider would undertake, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; b) engaging in activities that would be undertaken by a consultant for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; c) engaging in activities that would be undertaken by an auditor for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; d) engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role for an NDIS provider, the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or service to people with disability in the NDIS; e) providing or being involved or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS participant, of any support or supports under a participant’s plan; f) engaging in any work or providing or performing any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS provider, which may occasion any personal contact between them and an NDIS participant. This order takes effect from 5.00pm AEDT on 8 January 2026 and operates for 5 years across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Sigma SDA Management Pty Ltd
Registration Groups: 131 specialist disability accommodation only
Relevant information: In accordance with sections 73P(1)(a) and 73P(1)(d) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013, Sigma SDA Management Pty Ltd can no longer operate as registered NDIS provider, including providing specialist behaviour support, specialist disability accommodation, plan management services, any class of support or service to a person whose NDIS plan is managed by the NDIA and implementing regulated restrictive practices.The revocation decision was made on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner:• Reasonably believes that Sigma SDA Management Pty Ltd has contravened, or is proposing to contravene, section 73J of the NDIS Act by failing to comply with the NDIS Code of Conduct and NDIS Worker Screening requirements, and• Was no longer satisfied that Sigma SDA Management Pty Ltd is suitable to provide supports or services to people with disability.The revocation takes effect from 5:00pm AEST on 12 September 2025, across all states and territories of Australia.

Name: Ozihub Connect Pty Ltd (formerly known as Sunrise Aged Care and Disability Pty Ltd)
Registration Groups: 101 accommodation/tenancy assistance; 106 assistance in coordinating or managing life stages, transitions and supports; 107 assistance with daily personal activities; 108 assistance with travel/transport arrangements; 115 assistance with daily life tasks in a group or shared living arrangement; 116 innovative community participation; 117 development of daily living and life skills; 120 household tasks; 125 participation in community, social and civic activities; 136 group and centre-based activities
Relevant information: Ozihub Connect Pty was issued a suspension notice pursuant to Section 73N(1)(a)(i) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013. Ozihub Connect Pty Ltd cannot operate as registered NDIS provider for a period of 30 days from 5.00pm AEST 12 September 2025 until 11 October 2025, including providing specialist behaviour support, specialist disability accommodation, plan management services, any class of support or service to a person whose NDIS plan is managed by the NDIA and implementing regulated restrictive practices. The suspension was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner reasonably believes that Ozihub Connect Pty Ltd has contravened, is contravening, or is proposing to contravene the NDIS Act. More specifically, it is alleged that, Ozihub Connect Pty Ltd and its directors failed to comply with the the NDIS Code of Conduct and breached conditions of registration.The alleged conduct presents ongoing and significant risks to the safety and wellbeing of people with disability, particularly in preventing NDIS participants from utilising their NDIS plan funds to access necessary and critical supports and services.

Name: Independent Lifestyles Riverina Support Service Pty ltd
Relevant information: On 25 August 2025 a permanent banning order was made under section 73ZN(1)(b)(i) and 73ZN(1)(b)(ii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Independent Lifestyles Riverina Support Service Pty ltd from: 1. Engaging in activities of, and as, a NDIS provider;2. Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by a consultant for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services;3. Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by an auditor for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; and/or4. Engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities have a direct effect of the provision of NDIS funded supports or services to people with disability in the NDISThis Order takes effect on 12 September 2025 at 5.00pm AEST and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Helena Bond
Relevant information: On 26 August 2025 a banning order was issued to Helena Bond. The banning order is effective on 12 September 2025 at 5.00pm for a period of two (2) years and operates across all States and Territories of Australia. The banning order was made against Helena Bond under section 73ZN(2)(a)(i) of the NDIS Act, restricting them from:providing or personally being involved in the provision, to any NDIS participant, of any supports or services under a participant’s plan; orengaging in any work or providing or performing any supports or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider.

Name: Jimmy KOUFOS
Relevant information: On 27 August 2025, a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Jimmy Koufos from providing or managing NDIS supports, services or funding to people with disability in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), including but not limited to undertaking the following activities: engaging in activities that a member of the key personnel of an NDIS provider would undertake, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services;engaging in activities that would be undertaken by a consultant for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services;engaging in activities that would be undertaken by an auditor for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services;engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role for an NDIS provider, the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or service to people with disability in the NDIS.providing or being involved or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS participant, of any support or supports under a participant’s plan;providing or being involved or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS participant, of any service or services under a participant’s plan;engaging in any work or providing or performing any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS provider, which may occasion any personal contact between you and an NDIS participant.This Order takes effect on 12 September at 5.00pm AEST, for a period of two (2) years and operates across all States and Territories of Australia

Name: One Connection Disability Services Pty Ltd
Relevant information: On 1 September 2025, a permanent banning order was made under section 73ZN(1)(b)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013. The Banning Order permanently prohibits and restricts One Connection Disability Services Pty Ltd from being engaged in the following NDIS activities: engaging in any activities that would be delivering support and services to NDIS funded participants; engaging in activities that an NDIS provider would undertake, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; engaging in activities that would be undertaken as a consultant, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; engaging in activities that would be undertaken as an auditor for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; and engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role as a provider, to the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or services to people with disability in the NDIS.This banning order takes permanent effect on 5.00pm AEST on 12 September 2025 and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Imren Kenan Nouri

Name: Crescent Respite Facility Pty Ltd
Relevant information: On 29 August 2025, a permanent banning order was issued to Crescent Respite Facility Pty Ltd under section 73ZN(1)(b)(i), (iii) and (iv) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013.The Banning order prohibits Crescent Respite Facility Pty Ltd from being directly or indirectly engaged in the following NDIS activities:1) Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by a consultant for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; and2) Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by an auditor for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; and3) Engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or services to people with disability in the NDIS; and4) Engaging in the provision or management of NDIS supports, services or funding to people with disability in the NDISThis banning order takes effect on 12 September 2025 at 5.00pm AEST and is permanent and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Moaz (Jay) IBRAHIM

Name: Crescent Respite Facility Pty Ltd

The refusal will take effect 5.00pm AEST on 12 September 2025.

Name: Prestige Inhome Care Pty Ltd
Relevant information: On 1 September 2025, a Compliance Notice was issued to Prestige Inhome Care Pty Ltd.Prestige Inhome Care Pty Ltd, operating as a registered NDIS provider, may be contravening section 73J of the NDIS Act by breaching conditions of its registration determined by the National Disability Insurance Scheme Rules (refer to sections 73F(1)(c), 73F(2)(h) and 73H of the NDIS Act). In particular, Prestige Inhome Care Pty Ltd has contravened National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 (the Rules) and the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Incident Management and Reportable Incidents) Rules 2018 (Reportable Incident Rules).Prestige Inhome Care Pty Ltd is required to take action to address potential non-compliance, including a review of its incident management system, Risk and Human Resource Management processes and the development of a corrective action plan to address identified non-compliance.

Name: Australian Foundation For Disability (Afford)

Name: Hayam Mohamed Abdalla Ahmed Hammad trading as Sphinx Support Services
Relevant information: On the 26 August 2025 a compliance notice was issued to Hayam Mohamed Abdalla Ahmed Hammad trading as Sphinx Support Services.The compliance notice was issued on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner was aware of information that suggests that Hayam Mohamed Abdalla Ahmed Hammad trading as Sphinx Support Services has contravened section 73J of the NDIS Act, in that it has conditions of its registration determined by the National Disability Insurance Scheme Rules as prescribed in section 73F(1)(c) and 73H of the NDIS Act.Sphinx Support Services is required to take action to address non-compliance with regard to Governance and Operational Management, Risk Management and Human Resource Management as per Schedule 1 Clauses 9, 10 and 15 of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 and section 19 of the of the NDIS (Restrictive Practices and Behaviour Support) Rules 2018 (Behaviour Support Rules).

Name: Jacqueline Lenahan, trading as Jackies Support Services
Registration Groups: 102 assistance to access and maintain employment or higher education; 106 assistance in coordinating or managing life stages, transitions and supports; 107 assistance with daily personal activities; 108 assistance with travel/transport arrangements; 115 assistance with daily life tasks in a group or shared living arrangement; 116 innovative community participation; 117 development of daily living and life skills; 120 household tasks; 125 participation in community, social and civic activities; 136 group and centre-based activities
Relevant information: On 18 August 2025 a notice of revocation of registration was issued to Mrs Jacqueline Lenahan under sections 73P(1)(a) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013. The revocation takes effect from 26 August 2025.The revocation was issued on grounds that a delegate of the NDIS Commissioner has formed a reasonable belief that Mrs Jacqueline Lenahan:has contravened, is contravening, or is proposing to contravene, the NDIS Act by not complying with all applicable standards and other requirements of the NDIS Practice Standards (73F(2)(c).is no longer suitable to provide supports or services to people with disability.Mrs Jacqueline Lenahan can no longer operate as registered NDIS provider, including providing specialist behaviour support, specialist disability accommodation, plan management services, any class of support or service to a person whose NDIS plan is managed by the NDIA and implementing regulated restrictive practices.

Name: Leena Kumar
Relevant information: On 18 August 2025, a banning order originally made on 9 February 2024, was varied under section 73ZO of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013. The original banning order made under section 73ZN(2A) was varied from a 2-year banning order to a permanent banning order. The banning order permanently prohibits Ms Leena Kumar from engaging, directly or indirectly, inthe provision or management of NDIS supports, services or funding to people with disability in the National Disability Insurance Scheme, including but not limited to:1. engaging in activities that a member of the key personnel of an NDIS provider would undertake, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services;2. engaging in activities that would be undertaken by a consultant for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services;3. engaging in activities that would be undertaken by an auditor for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services;4. engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role for an NDIS provider, the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or service to people with disability in the NDIS;5. providing or being involved or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS participant, of any support or supports under a participant’s plan;6. engaging in any work or providing or performing any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS provider, which may occasion any personal contact between you and an NDIS participant.The banning order is effective from 5:00pm AEST 25 August 2025 and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Ali Abdul Rahman
Relevant information: On 11 August 2025 a permanent banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(i)(ii)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Ali Abdul Rahman from engaging, directly or indirectly, in the provision or management of NDIS supports, services or funding to people with disability in the National Disability Insurance Scheme, including but not limited to:• Engaging in activities of as a NDIS provider; and• Delivering supports or services to NDIS funded participants; and• Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by a consultant for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; and• Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by an auditor for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; and• Engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role for a NDIS provider, the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or service to people with disability in the NDIS. This banning order takes effect on 25 August 2025 at 5:00pm (AEST) and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Helen Hodgetts

Name: Ashley David Liddicoat
Relevant information: On 20 June 2025 a banning order was made under 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the NDIS Act of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013. The banning order prohibits Ashley David Liddicoat from: engaging in any activities that would be delivering supports and services to NDIS funded participants under 16 years, or engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role as a provider or worker, to the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or services to people with disability in the NDIS who are under 16 years. The banning order takes effect on 27 June 2025 at 5:00pm AEST for a period of three (3) years and operates across all states and territories of Australia.

Name: Embrace Community Care Pty Ltd
Relevant information: On the 26 June 2025 a compliance notice was issued to Embrace Community Care Pty Ltd. The notice requires Embrace Community Care Pty Ltd to take certain actions to address possible non-compliance with clauses 10 and 22 of Schedule 1 of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018 (Practice Standards Rules) and sections 6(c) and 6(f) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Code of Conduct) Rules 2018.

Name: Amanda McDonnell
Relevant information: On 18 June 2025 a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(i) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Amanda McDonnell from engaging in the provision or management of NDIS supports, services or funding to people with disability in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), including but not limited to: Delivering supports or services to NDIS funded participants; Engaging in activities that a member of the key personnel of a NDIS provider would undertake, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or service; Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by a consultant for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or service; Engaging in activities that would be undertaken by an auditor for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; and Engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or services to people with disability in the NDIS.The banning order is effective on 24 June 2025 at 5:00pm (AEST) for a period of five (5) years and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Rosemary Jane Hughes
Relevant information: On 2 June 2025 a banning order was made under 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Rosemary Jane Hughes from:Being engaged, or otherwise employed, in any role where they are responsible for the provision of any NDIS supports or services to NDIS participants; andHaving unsupervised contact with NDIS participants as part of the provision of NDIS supports or services. In this instance: Contact includes physical contact, face-to-face contact, oral communication, written communication and electronic communication; andSupervision means direct and in-person observation. The banning order takes effect on 23 June 2025 at 5:00PM ACST for a period of four (4) years across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Muhammad Ali
Relevant information: On 2 June 2025, a banning order was made under 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 restricting Muhammad Ali from being involved in providing NDIS supports or services to NDIS funded participants under the age of 18 years. The banning order takes effect on 23 June 2025 at 5:00PM AEST for a period of four (4) years across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Anthony John Francis Ward
Relevant information: On 2 June 2025 a banning order was made under 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Anthony John Francis Ward from:Being involved in any NDIS supports and services to NDIS participants involving direct contact; andEngaging in the provision of any NDIS funded supports or services, whether paid or unpaid for or on behalf of any NDIS provider, which may include:o Access to any NDIS participant’s sensitive or financial information (such as banking details); ando Provision of any activities related to the management or use of NDIS plan funds, including handling of financial information relating to NDIS plans. This Order takes effect on 23 June 2025 at 5:00pm (AWST) for a period of five (5) years and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Raju Lamichhane
Relevant information: On 18 March 2025, a two-year banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(i) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013. The banning order prohibits Raju Lamichhane from:1. providing or personally being involved in the provision, to any NDIS participant, of any support or supports under a participant’s plan; or2.engaging in any work or providing or performing any supports or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider.The banning order is effective from 09:00am (AEDT) on 4 April 2025 and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Shane Darren Carle
Relevant information: On 3 April 2025 a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013. The banning order prohibits Shane Darren Carle from:1. Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any support or supports under a Participant’s Plan;2. Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any service or services under a Participant’s Plan.The banning order is for a period of five (5) years, effective from 5.00pm AWST on 4 April 2025, across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Maria Crestani
Relevant information: On 4 April 2025 a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013. The banning order restricts/prohibits Maria Crestani from being engaged in the following activities related to the provision of NDIS funded supports and services to NDIS participants for a period of five (5) years:Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any support or under a Participant’s PlanProvide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any service or services under a Participant’s PlanEngage in any work or provide or perform any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider, which may occasion any personal contact between you and a NDIS Participant.The banning order is for a period of five (5) years effective from 5:00pm AEST on 4 April 2025, across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Nicholas (Nick) Pefkos
Relevant information: On 3 April 2025, a banning order originally issued on 16 December 2024 was varied. The banning order was varied under section 100 of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Mr Nicholas (Nick) Pefkos from engaging in the provision of NDIS supports and services to people with disability in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), including but not limited to:Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any support or supports under a Participant’s Plan;Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any service services under a Participant’s PlanEngage in any work or provide or perform any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider, which may occasion any personal contact between you and a NDIS Participant.This banning order is made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(i) of the NDIS Act and takes effect on 3 April 2025 at 5:00 pm (AEDT). The order remains in force permanently, across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Michelle Grant

Name: Beverley Faith Rita Duffin
Relevant information: On 19 March 2025 a banning order was made under 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting BEVERLEY Faith Rita DUFFIN from:1. Being engaged, or otherwise employed, in any role where she is solely responsible for the provision of any NDIS supports or services to NDIS participants; and 2. Having unsupervised contact with NDIS participants as part of the provision of NDIS supports or services. In this instance: Contact includes physical contact, face-to-face contact, oral communication, written communication and electronic communication; and Supervision means direct and in-person observation. The banning order is effective from 05:00pm ACDT on 28 March 2025 for a period of four (4) years.

Name: Lisa Robson
Relevant information: On 18 March 2025 a conditional banning order was made under Section 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013, this order permits and restricts Lisa Robson from the following activities:Permitted Activities:May provide the following supports only to their existing clientele:• Daily personal activities as defined in the NDIS Support Catalogue;• Community access and social participation activities;• Non-clinical daily living support.Prohibited Activities:Must not:• Provide any high intensity daily personal activities as defined in Schedule 2 of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018;• Engage in any clinical care activities, including but not limited to medication administration or management;• Provide care for any clientele with a mealtime management or behaviour support plan in place.The banning order is effective from 5:00pm AEST on 28 March 2025 for a period of five (5) years and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

Name: Robert Curley
Relevant information: On 18 February 2025, a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(i) the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013, permanently prohibiting Robert Curley from using any of the restrictive practices as described and listed by the Victorian Senior Practitioners Office (VSPO) as being prohibited.These prohibited restrictive practices include the following as per the directions of the VSPO:(a) the use of prone restraint (subduing a person by forcing them into a face-down position)(b) the use of supine restraint (subduing a person by forcing them into a face-up position)(c) pin downs (subduing a person by holding down their limbs or any part of the body, such as their arms or legs)(d) basket holds (subduing a person by wrapping your arm/s around their upper and or lower body)(e) takedown techniques (subduing a person by forcing them to free-fall to the floor or by forcing them to fall to the floor with support)(f) any physical restraint that has the purpose or effect of restraining or inhibiting a person’s respiratory or digestive function(g) any physical restraint that has the effect of pushing the person’s head forward onto their chest(h) any physical restraint that has the purpose or effect of compelling a person’s compliance through the infliction of pain, hyperextension of joints, or by applying pressure to the chest or joints This order takes effect from 5pm AEDST on the 21 February 2025.
